solution.answer: $H_0 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_1 \cong \mathbb{Z}/2,\quad H_2 = 0,\quad H_3 \cong \mathbb{Z},\quad H_n = 0 \text{ for } n \geq 4.$
steps: For $\mathbb{R}P^n$, the CW structure has one cell $e^k$ in each dimension $0 \leq k \leq n$. The boundary map is $d_k = \deg(\text{attaching map}) = 1 + (-1)^k$.
For $n = 3$: chain complex $0 \to C_3 \xrightarrow{d_3=0} C_2 \xrightarrow{d_2=2} C_1 \xrightarrow{d_1=0} C_0 \to 0$ with each $C_k = \mathbb{Z}$.
$H_0 = \mathbb{Z}$. Note $\mathbb{R}P^3$ is a closed orientable 3-manifold (it is the group $SO(3)$), consistent with $H_3 = \mathbb{Z}$.
